title: Replace pairwise rank side-model with true groupwise CatBoost ranking on race-lap choice sets
summary: The target is a next-lap decision among cars sharing the same race/lap context, so within-lap ordering matters.
rationale: The target is a next-lap decision among cars sharing the same race/lap context, so within-lap ordering matters. CatBoost supports groupwise ranking objectives beyond simple pairwise loss, including `YetiRankPairwise`, `QuerySoftMax`, `LambdaMart`, and `QueryAUC`, which better match choice-set structure than the current XGBoost `rank:pairwise` add-on.
implementation_hint: Create groups as `Year_Race_LapNumber`. Train a ranking model only on rows where at least one positive exists in the group, optionally weighting groups by pit-wave size or late-race importance. Blend its rank-normalized OOF predictions with the binary classifiers rather than raw probabilities.
expected_effect: A more complementary ensemble member than current pairwise XGB, especially for pit-wave laps where relative ordering inside a lap matters most.
risk: Ranking objectives can underperform if many groups have zero positives, and some CatBoost ranking modes are CPU-only/slower. Needs careful group filtering to avoid noisy supervision.
